AllConnections.note: For example, may be used to indicate all “friends” of the Party. Note that “group” scope is also assumed.
AllConnections.definition: Specifies that the scope of the relationship is all of the first-level connections of the Party.
AllConnections.label: All First-Level Connections

Ticket.label: Ticket
Ticket.note: A Ticket Policy MUST contain a target Asset and at least one of a Permission or Prohibition rule.  The Ticket MAY contain the Party with Assigner function and MUST NOT contain an Assignee. The Ticket Policy will grant the terms of the Policy to the holder of that Ticket. The holder of the Ticket MAY remain unknown or MAY have to be identified at some later stage.
Ticket.definition: A Policy that grants the holder a Rule over an Asset from an assigner.

ShareAlike.label: Share Alike
ShareAlike.definition: Derivative works be licensed under the same terms or compatible terms as the original work.
ShareAlike.note: This term is defined by Creative Commons.

extractWord.definition: The act of extracting (replicating) unchanged words from the asset.
extractWord.note: This original term and URI from the ONIX specification should be used: http://www.editeur.org/onix-pl/extract-word .
extractWord.label: Extract word

Privacy.definition: A Policy that expresses a Rule over an Asset containing personal information.
Privacy.label: Privacy Policy
Privacy.note: A Privacy Policy  MUST contain a target Asset, a Party with Assigner  is, a Party with Assignee function, and at least one of a Permission or Prohibition rule that MUST include a Duty. The target Asset SHOULD contain or relate to personal information about the Assignee. The Duty MUST describe obligations on the Assigner about managing the Asset. The Assignee is being granted the terms of the Privacy policy from the Assigner.

Distribution.note: This term is defined by Creative Commons.
Distribution.definition: Distribution, public display, and publicly performance.
Distribution.label: Distribution

absoluteTemporalPosition.definition: The absolute temporal positions in a media stream the target Asset has to fit.
absoluteTemporalPosition.note:
Use with Actions including the target Asset in a larger media stream. The fragment part of a Media Fragment URI (https://www.w3.org/TR/media-frags/) may be used for the right operand. See the Left Operand realativeTemporalPosition.
Example: The MP3 music file must be positioned between second 192 and 250 of the temporal length of a stream.
absoluteTemporalPosition.label: Absolute Temporal Asset Position

compensatingParty.definition: The Party that is the provider of the compensation.
compensatingParty.note: Maybe specified as part of the compensate duty action.
compensatingParty.label: Compensating Party

relation.note: Sub-properties of relation are used to define the nature of that link.
relation.label: Relation
relation.definition: Relation is an abstract property which creates an explicit link between an Action and an Asset.

acceptTracking.label: Accept Tracking
acceptTracking.definition: To accept that the use of the Asset may be tracked.
acceptTracking.note: The collected information may be tracked by the Assigner, or may link to a Party with the role 'trackingParty' function.

attribute.label: Attribute
attribute.definition: To attribute the use of the Asset.
attribute.note: May link to an Asset with the attribution information. May link to a Party with the  role “attributedParty” function.

consentedParty.note: Maybe specified as part of the obtainConsent action.
consentedParty.label: Consented Party
consentedParty.definition: The Party who obtains the consent.

informedParty.note: Maybe specified as part of the inform action.
informedParty.definition: The Party to be informed of all uses.
informedParty.label: Informed Party

compensatedParty.definition: The Party is the recipient of the compensation.
compensatedParty.label: Compensated Party
compensatedParty.note: Maybe specified as part of the compensate duty action.

invalid.note: Used to indicate the policy is void for Conflict Strategy.
invalid.label: Void Policy

profile.definition: The identifier(s) of an ODRL Profile that the Policy conforms to.
profile.label: Profile
profile.note: The profile property is mandatory if the Policy is using an ODRL Profile.

lease.label: Lease
lease.definition: The act of making available the asset to a third-party for a fixed period of time with exchange of value.

gt.label: Greater than
gt.definition: Indicating that a given value is greater than the right operand of the Constraint.

copy.definition: The act of making an exact reproduction of the asset.
copy.label: Copy

gteq.definition: Indicating that a given value is greater than or equal to the right operand of the Constraint.
gteq.label: Greater than or equal to

include.definition: To include other related assets in the Asset.
include.note: For example: bio picture must be included in the attribution. Use of a relation sub-property is required for the related assets.
include.label: Include

ignore.label: Ignore Undefined Actions
ignore.definition: The Action is to be ignored and is not part of the policy – and the policy remains valid.
ignore.note: Used to support actions not known to the policy system.

obtainConsent.note: May be used as a Duty to ensure that the Assigner or a Party is authorized to approve such actions on a case-by-case basis. May link to a Party with the role “consentingParty” function.
obtainConsent.definition: To obtain verifiable consent to perform the requested action in relation to the Asset.
obtainConsent.label: Obtain Consent

relativePosition.label: Relative Asset Position
relativePosition.definition: A point in space or time defined with coordinates relative to full measures the positioning of the target Asset.
relativePosition.note: Example: The upper left corner of a picture may be constrained to a specific position of the canvas rendering it.

Duty.label: Duty
Duty.definition: The obligation to perform an Action

product.note: Example: May only be used in the XYZ Magazine.
product.definition: Category of product or service setting a context for exercising the action of the Rule.
product.label: Product Context

spatialCoordinates.definition: A set of coordinates setting the borders of a geospatial area used for exercising the action of the Rule. The coordinates MUST include longitude and latitude, they MAY include altitude and the geodetic datum.
spatialCoordinates.label: Geospatial Coordinates
spatialCoordinates.note: The default values are the altitude of earth's surface at this location and the WGS 84 datum.

assignerOf.definition: Identifies an ODRL Policy for which the identified Party undertakes the assigner functional role.
assignerOf.label: Assigner Of
assignerOf.note: When assignerOf has been asserted between a metadata expression and an ODRL Policy, the Party being identified MUST be inferred to undertake the assigner functional role of all the Rules of that Policy.

install.label: Install
install.definition: To load the computer program Asset onto a storage device which allows operating or running the Asset.

commercialize.definition: The act of using the asset in a business environment.
commercialize.label: Commercialize

includedIn.label: Included In
includedIn.definition: An Action transitively asserts that another Action that encompasses its operational semantics.
includedIn.note: The purpose is to explicitly assert that the semantics of the referenced instance of an other Action encompasses (includes) the semantics of this instance of Action. The includedIn property is transitive, and as such, the Actions form ancestor relationships.

contractedParty.label: Contracted Party
contractedParty.definition: The Party who is being contracted.

DerivativeWorks.label: Derivative Works
DerivativeWorks.note: This term is defined by Creative Commons.

display.label: Display
display.definition: To create a static and transient rendition of an Asset.
display.note: For example, displaying an image on a screen. If the action is to be performed to a wider audience than just the Assignees, then the Recipient constraint is recommended to be used.

function.definition: Function is an abstract property whose sub-properties define the functional roles which may be fulfilled by a party in relation to a Rule.
function.label: Function

consentingParty.label: Consenting Party
consentingParty.note: Maybe specified as part of the obtainConsent action.
consentingParty.definition: The Party to obtain consent from.

deliveryChannel.label: Delivery Channel
deliveryChannel.note: Example: the asset may be distributed only on mobile networks.
deliveryChannel.definition: The delivery channel used for exercising the action of the Rule.

reproduce.label: Reproduce
reproduce.definition: To make duplicate copies the Asset in any material form.

print.definition: To create a tangible and permanent rendition of an Asset.
print.note: For example, creating a permanent, fixed (static), and directly perceivable representation of the Asset, such as printing onto paper.
print.label: Print

refinement.definition: Constraint used to refine the semantics of an Action, or Party/Asset Collection
refinement.note: Example: the Action print is only permitted on 50% of the asset.
refinement.label: Refinement

Assertion.note: For example, a party (an assignee or assigner) can claim what terms they have over an Asset. An Assertion Policy does not grant such permissions/prohibitions but only asserts the parties claims. An Assetion Policy  MUST contain a target Asset, a Party with any functional role, and at least one of a Permission or Prohibition rule.
Assertion.label: Assertion
Assertion.definition: A Policy that asserts a Rule over an Asset from parties.

assigneeOf.label: Assignee Of
assigneeOf.note: When assigneeOf has been asserted between a metadata expression and an ODRL Policy, the Party being identified MUST be inferred to undertake the assignee functional role of all the Rules of that Policy.
assigneeOf.definition: Identifies an ODRL Policy for which the identified Party undertakes the assignee functional role.

Operator.label: Operator
Operator.note: Instances of the Operator class representing relational operators.

nextPolicy.label: Next Policy
nextPolicy.definition: To grant the specified Policy to a third party for their use of the Asset.

isA.definition: A set-based operator indicating that a given value is an instance of the right operand of the Constraint.
isA.label: Is a

Prohibition.label: Prohibition
Prohibition.definition: The inability to perform an Action over an Asset.

Party.label: Party
Party.note: The Party entity could be a person, group of people, organisation, or agent. An agent is a person or thing that takes an active role or produces a specified effect. To describe more details about the Party, it is recommended to use W3C vCard Ontology [[vcard-rdf]] or FOAF Vocabulary [[foaf]].
Party.definition: An entity or a collection of entities that undertake Roles in a Rule.

trackingParty.note: May be specified as part of the acceptTracking action.
trackingParty.label: Tracking Party
trackingParty.definition: The Party who is tracking usage.

AssetCollection.label: Asset Collection
AssetCollection.definition: An Asset that is collection of individual resources

Constraint.label: Constraint
Constraint.definition: A boolean expression that refines the semantics of an Action and Party/Asset Collection or declare the conditions applicable to a Rule.

inform.label: Inform
inform.note: May link to a Party with the role 'informedParty' function.
inform.definition: To inform that an action has been performed on or in relation to the Asset.

language.label: Language
language.definition: A natural language used by the target Asset.
language.note: Example: the asset can only be translated into Greek. Must use [[bcp47]] codes for language values.

version.note: Example: Single Paperback or Multiple Issues or version 2.0 or higher.
version.label: Version
version.definition: The version of the target Asset.

writeTo.definition: The act of adding data to the Asset.
writeTo.label: Write to

obligation.note: The Duty is a requirement which must be fulfilled.
obligation.definition: Relates an individual Duty to a Policy.
obligation.label: Obligation

LeftOperand.definition: Left operand for a constraint expression.
LeftOperand.label: Left Operand
LeftOperand.note: Instances of the LeftOperand class are used as the leftOperand of a Constraint.

stream.label: Stream
stream.definition: To deliver the Asset in real-time.
stream.note: The Asset maybe utilised in real-time as it is being delivered. If the action is to be performed to a wider audience than just the Assignees, then the Recipient constraint is recommended to be used.

aggregate.definition: To use the Asset or parts of it as part of a composite collection.
aggregate.label: Aggregate

transform.label: Transform
transform.note: Typically used to convert the Asset into a different format for consumption on/transfer to a third party system.
transform.definition: To convert the Asset into a different format.

Agreement.definition: A Policy that grants the assignee a Rule over an Asset from an assigner.
Agreement.label: Agreement
Agreement.note: An Agreement Policy MUST contain at least one Permission or Prohibition rule, a Party with Assigner function, and a Party with Assignee function (in the same Permission or Prohibition). The Agreement Policy will grant the terms of the Policy from the Assigner to the Assignee.

duty.label: Has Duty
duty.definition: Relates an individual Duty to a Permission.
duty.note: A Duty is a pre-condition which must be fulfilled in order to receive the Permission.

uid.label: Unique Identifier
uid.note: Used by the Policy, Rule, Asset, Party, Constraint, and Logical Constraint Classes.

isAllOf.label: Is all of
isAllOf.definition: A set-based operator indicating that a given value is all of the right operand of the Constraint.

use.label: Use
use.note: Use is the most generic action for all non-third-party usage. More specific types of the use action can be expressed by more targetted actions.

index.definition: To record the Asset in an index.
index.note: For example, to include a link to the Asset in a search engine database.
index.label: Index

extract.label: Extract
extract.note: A new asset is created and may have very little in common with the original Asset. (Note that the notion of whether or not the change is significant enough to qualify as a new asset is subjective). To the extracted Asset a next policy may be applied.
extract.definition: To extract parts of the Asset and to use it as a new Asset.

unit.definition: The unit of measurement of the value of the rightOperand or rightOperandReference of a Constraint.
unit.label: Unit

archive.note: Temporal constraints may be used for temporal conditions.
archive.definition: To store the Asset (in a non-transient form).
archive.label: Archive

distribute.definition: To supply the Asset to third-parties.
distribute.label: Distribute
distribute.note: It is recommended to use nextPolicy to express the allowable usages by third-parties.

Offer.note: An Offer Policy MUST contain at least one Permission or Prohibition rule and a Party with Assigner function (in the same Permission or Prohibition). The Offer Policy MAY contain a Party with Assignee function, but MUST not grant any privileges to that Party.
Offer.definition: A Policy that proposes a Rule over an Asset from an assigner.
Offer.label: Offer

system.definition: An identified computing system used for exercising the action of the Rule.
system.label: System

Set.label: Set
Set.definition: A Policy that expresses a Rule over an Asset.
Set.note: A Set Policy MUST contain a target Asset, and at least one Rule. A Set Policy is the default Policy subclass. The Set is aimed at scenarios where there is an open criteria for the semantics of the policy expressions and typically refined by other systems/profiles that process the information at a later time. No privileges are granted to any Party (if defined).

appendTo.definition: The act of appending data to the Asset without modifying the Asset in any other way.
appendTo.label: Append To

attachPolicy.label: Attach policy
attachPolicy.definition: The act of keeping the policy notice with the asset.

implies.note: The property asserts that an instance of Action entails that the other instance of Action is not prohibited.
implies.definition: An Action asserts that another Action is not prohibited to enable its operational semantics.
implies.label: Implies

pay.definition: The act of paying a financial amount to a party for use of the asset.
pay.label: Pay

assigner.definition: The Party is the issuer of the Rule.
assigner.label: Assigner

inheritFrom.note: The child policy will inherit Rules from the parent policy
inheritFrom.definition: Relates a (child) policy to another (parent) policy from which terms are inherited.
inheritFrom.label: Inherits From

digitize.definition: To produce a digital copy of (or otherwise digitize) the Asset from its analogue form.
digitize.label: Digitize

translate.label: Translate
translate.note: A new derivative Asset is created by that action.
translate.definition: To translate the original natural language of an Asset into another natural language.

core.definition: Identifier for the ODRL Core Profile
core.label: ODRL Core Profile

prohibition.definition: Relates an individual Prohibition to a Policy.
prohibition.label: Has Prohibition

payeeParty.label: Payee Party
payeeParty.definition: The Party is the recipient of the payment.

absolutePosition.definition: A point in space or time defined with absolute coordinates for the positioning of the target Asset.
absolutePosition.label: Absolute Asset Position
absolutePosition.note: Example: The upper left corner of a picture may be constrained to a specific position of the canvas rendering it.

attributingParty.definition: The Party who undertakes the attribution.
attributingParty.label: Attributing Party
attributingParty.note: Maybe specified as part of the attribute action.

proximity.label: proximity
proximity.note: This original term and URI from the OMA specification should be used: http://www.openmobilealliance.com/oma-dd/proximity .
proximity.definition: An value indicating the closeness or nearness.

percentage.definition: A percentage amount of the target Asset relevant for exercising the action of the Rule. Right operand value MUST be an xsd:decimal from 0 to 100.
percentage.note: Example: Extract less than or equal to 50%.
percentage.label: Asset Percentage

relativeTemporalPosition.label: Relative Temporal Asset Position
relativeTemporalPosition.definition: A point in space or time defined with coordinates relative to full measures the positioning of the target Asset.
relativeTemporalPosition.note:
See also Absolute Temporal Asset Position.
Example: The MP3 music file must be positioned between the positions at 33% and 48% of the temporal length of a stream.

target.label: Target
target.definition: The target property indicates the Asset that is the primary subject to which the Rule action directly applies.

systemDevice.note: Example: The system device can be identified by a unique code created from the used hardware.
systemDevice.definition: An identified computing system or computing device used for exercising the action of the Rule.
systemDevice.label: System Device

hasPolicy.note: The Asset being identified MUST be inferred to be the target Asset of all of the Rules of the Policy.
hasPolicy.label: Target Policy
hasPolicy.definition: Identifies an ODRL Policy for which the identified Asset is the target Asset to all the Rules.

relativeSize.label: Relative Asset Size
relativeSize.note: Example: The image can be resized in width to a maximum of 200%. Note: See the Left Operand absoluteSize.
relativeSize.definition: Measure(s) of one or two axes for 2D-objects or measure(s) of one to tree axes for 3D-objects - expressed as percentages of full values - of the target Asset.

informingParty.label: Informing Party
informingParty.note: Maybe specified as part of the inform action.
informingParty.definition: The Party who provides the inform use data.

Permission.definition: The ability to perform an Action over an Asset.
Permission.label: Permission

isAnyOf.label: Is any of
isAnyOf.definition: A set-based operator indicating that a given value is any of the right operand of the Constraint.

spatial.note:
A code value for the area and source of the code must be presented in the Right Operand.
Example: the [[iso3166]] Country Codes or the Getty Thesaurus of Geographic Names.
spatial.label: Geospatial Named Area
spatial.definition: A named and identified geospatial area with defined borders which is used for exercising the action of the Rule. An IRI MUST be used to represent this value.

undefined.definition: Relates the strategy used for handling undefined actions to a Policy.
undefined.label: Handle Undefined Term
undefined.note: If no strategy is specified, the default is invalid.

inheritRelation.note: For example, this may indicate the business scenario, such as subscription, or prior arrangements between the parties (that are not machine representable).
inheritRelation.label: Inherit Relation

media.note: Example media types: electronic, print, advertising, marketing. Note: The used type should not be an IANA MediaType as they are focused on technical characteristics.
media.definition: Category of a media asset setting a context for exercising the action of the Rule.
media.label: Media Context

Request.label: Request
Request.note: A Request Policy  MUST contain a target Asset, a Party with Assignee function, and at least one of a Permission or Prohibition rule. The Request MAY also contain the Party with Assigner function if this is known. No privileges are granted to any Party.
Request.definition: A Policy that proposes a Rule over an Asset from an assignee.

consequence.note: The consequence property is utilised to express the repercussions of not fulfilling an agreed Policy obligation or duty for a Permission. If either of these fails to be fulfilled, then this will result in the consequence Duty also becoming a new requirement, meaning that the original obligation or duty, as well as the consequence Duty must all be fulfilled
consequence.definition: Relates a Duty to another Duty, the latter being a consequence of not fulfilling the former.
consequence.label: Consequence

export.definition: The act of transforming the asset into a new form.
export.label: Export

lteq.label: Less than or equal to
lteq.definition: Indicating that a given value is less than or equal to the right operand of the Constraint.

annotate.label: Annotate
annotate.definition: To add explanatory notations/commentaries to the Asset without modifying the Asset in any other way.

AllGroups.label: All Group Connections
AllGroups.note: For example, may be used to indicate all groups that the Party is a member of. Note that “group” scope is also assumed.
AllGroups.definition: Specifies that the scope of the relationship is all of the group connections of the Party.

All2ndConnections.definition: Specifies that the scope of the relationship is all of the second-level connections to the Party.
All2ndConnections.note: For example, may be used to indicate all “friends of friends” of the Party. Note that “group” scope is also assumed.
All2ndConnections.label: All Second-level Connections

eq.label: Equal to
eq.definition: Indicating that a given value equals the right operand of the Constraint.

leftOperand.definition: The left operand in a constraint expression.
leftOperand.label: Has Left Operand

count.definition: Numeric count of executions of the action of the Rule.
count.label: Count

operand.note: Sub-properties of operand are used for Logical Constraints.
operand.label: Operand
operand.definition: Operand is an abstract property for a logical relationship.

trackedParty.label: Tracked Party
trackedParty.definition: The Party whose usage is being tracked.
trackedParty.note: May be specified as part of the acceptTracking action.

Attribution.note: This term is defined by Creative Commons.
Attribution.definition: Credit be given to copyright holder and/or author.
Attribution.label: Attribution

LogicalConstraint.definition: A logical expression that refines the semantics of an Action and Party/Asset Collection or declare the conditions applicable to a Rule.
LogicalConstraint.label: Logical Constraint

virtualLocation.definition: An identified location of the IT communication space which is relevant for exercising the action of the Rule.
virtualLocation.note: Example: an Internet domain or IP address range.
virtualLocation.label: Virtual IT Communication Location

ensureExclusivity.definition: To ensure that the Rule on the Asset is exclusive.
ensureExclusivity.label: Ensure Exclusivity
ensureExclusivity.note: If used as a Duty, the assignee should be explicitly indicated as the party that is ensuring the exclusivity of the Rule.

industry.definition: A defined industry sector setting a context for exercising the action of the Rule.
industry.label: Industry Context

Rule.definition: An abstract concept that represents the common characteristics of Permissions, Prohibitions, and Duties.
Rule.label: Rule

status.definition: the value generated from the leftOperand action or a value related to the leftOperand set as the reference for the comparison.
status.label: Status

sell.label: Sell
sell.definition: To transfer the ownership of the Asset to a third party with compensation and while deleting the original asset.

resolution.label: Rendition Resolution
resolution.definition: Resolution of the rendition of the target Asset.
resolution.note: Example: the image may be printed at 1200dpi.

conflict.note: If no strategy is specified, the default is invalid.
conflict.label: Handle Policy Conflicts
conflict.definition: The conflict-resolution strategy for a Policy.

attributedParty.note: Maybe specified as part of the attribute action.
attributedParty.label: Attributed Party

CommercialUse.note: This term is defined by Creative Commons.
CommercialUse.label: Commercial Use

watermark.definition: To apply a watermark to the Asset.
watermark.label: Watermark

license.definition: The act of granting the right to use the asset to a third-party.
license.label: License

absoluteSize.label: Absolute Asset Size
absoluteSize.note: Example: The image can be resized in width to a maximum of 1000px.
absoluteSize.definition: Measure(s) of one or two axes for 2D-objects or measure(s) of one to tree axes for 3D-objects of the target Asset.

recipient.note: The Right Operand must identify one or more specific Parties or category/ies of the Party.
recipient.definition: The party receiving the result/outcome of exercising the action of the Rule.
recipient.label: Recipient

absoluteSpatialPosition.definition: The absolute spatial positions of four corners of a rectangle on a 2D-canvas or the eight corners of a cuboid in a 3D-space for the target Asset to fit.
absoluteSpatialPosition.label: Absolute Spatial Asset Position
absoluteSpatialPosition.note: Example: The upper left corner of a picture may be constrained to a specific position of the canvas rendering it. Note: see also the Left Operand Relative Spatial Asset Position.

rightOperand.note: When used with set-based operators, a list of values may be used.
rightOperand.definition: The value of the right operand in a constraint expression.
rightOperand.label: Has Right Operand

rightOperandReference.note: An IRI that MUST be dereferenced to obtain the actual right operand value. When used with set-based operators, a list of IRIs may be used
rightOperandReference.definition: A reference to a web resource providing the value for the right operand of a Constraint.
rightOperandReference.label: Has Right Operand Reference

permission.label: Has Permission
permission.definition: Relates an individual Permission to a Policy.

extractPage.note: This original term and URI from the ONIX specification should be used: http://www.editeur.org/onix-pl/extract-page .
extractPage.label: Extract page
extractPage.definition: The act of extracting (replicating) unchanged pages from the asset.

source.note: Used by AssetCollection and PartyCollection when constraints are applied.
source.label: Source

lend.definition: The act of making available the asset to a third-party for a fixed period of time without exchange of value.
lend.label: Lend

present.note: The asset can be performed (or communicated) in public.
present.label: Present

delayPeriod.label: Delay Period
delayPeriod.definition: A time delay period prior to exercising the action of the Rule. The point in time triggering this period MAY be defined by another temporal Constraint combined by a Logical Constraint (utilising the odrl:andSequence operand). Right operand value MUST be an xsd:duration as defined by [[xmlschema11-2]].
delayPeriod.note:
Only the eq, gt, gteq operators SHOULD be used.
Example: delayPeriod eq P60M indicates a delay of 60 Minutes before exercising the action.

support.definition: The Action is to be supported as part of the policy – and the policy remains valid.
support.note: Used to support actions not known to the policy system.
support.label: Support Undefined Actions

grantUse.definition: To grant the use of the Asset to third parties.
grantUse.label: Grant Use
grantUse.note: This action enables the assignee to create policies for the use of the Asset for third parties. The nextPolicy is recommended to be agreed with the third party. Use of temporal constraints is recommended.

transfer.label: Transfer Ownership
transfer.definition: To transfer the ownership of the Asset in perpetuity.

write.definition: The act of writing to the Asset.
write.label: Write

relativeSpatialPosition.label: Relative Spatial Asset Position
relativeSpatialPosition.note: See also Absolute Spatial Asset Position.
relativeSpatialPosition.definition: The relative spatial positions - expressed as percentages of full values - of four corners of a rectangle on a 2D-canvas or the eight corners of a cuboid in a 3D-space of the target Asset.

uninstall.note: The Asset is no longer accessible to the assignees after it has been used.
uninstall.definition: To unload and delete the computer program Asset from a storage device and disable its readiness for operation.
uninstall.label: Uninstall

give.label: Give
give.definition: To transfer the ownership of the Asset to a third party without compensation and while deleting the original asset.

reviewPolicy.note: Used when human intervention is required to review the Policy. May link to an Asset which represents the full Policy information.
reviewPolicy.definition: To review the Policy applicable to the Asset.
reviewPolicy.label: Review Policy

Group.definition: Specifies that the scope of the relationship is the defined group with multiple individual members.
Group.label: Group

hasPart.label: Has part
hasPart.definition: A set-based operator indicating that a given value contains the right operand of the Constraint.

extractChar.label: Extract character
extractChar.definition: The act of extracting (replicating) unchanged characters from the asset.
extractChar.note: This original term and URI from the ONIX specification should be used: http://www.editeur.org/onix-pl/extract-char .

or.definition: The relation is satisfied when at least one of the Constraints is satisfied.
or.note: This property MUST only be used for Logical Constraints, and the list of operand values MUST be Constraint instances.
or.label: Or

contractingParty.label: Contracting Party
contractingParty.definition: The Party who is offering the contract.

AssetScope.note: Instances of the AssetScope class represent the terms for the scope property of Assets.
AssetScope.label: Asset Scope

creator: Michael Steidl
Renato Iannella
Stuart Myles
Víctor Rodríguez-Doncel
http://www.w3.org/ns/odrl/2/.contributor: W3C Permissions & Obligations Expression Working Group
http://www.w3.org/ns/odrl/2/.label: ODRL Version 2.2
http://www.w3.org/ns/odrl/2/.comment: This is the RDF ontology for ODRL Version 2.2.
http://www.w3.org/ns/odrl/2/.description: The ODRL Vocabulary and Expression defines a set of concepts and terms (the vocabulary) and encoding mechanism (the expression) for permissions and obligations statements describing digital content usage based on the ODRL Information Model.

concurrentUse.definition: To create multiple copies of the Asset that are being concurrently used.
concurrentUse.label: Concurrent Use

Action.label: Action
Action.note: Actions may be allowed by Permissions, disallowed by Prohibitions, or made mandatory by Duties.

operator.definition: The operator function applied to operands of a Constraint
operator.label: Has Operator

PartyCollection.definition: A Party that is a group of individual entities
PartyCollection.label: Party Collection

synchronize.definition: To use the Asset in timed relations with media (audio/visual) elements of another Asset.
synchronize.label: Synchronize

execute.label: Execute
execute.definition: To run the computer program Asset.
execute.note: For example, machine executable code or Java such as a game or application.

timedCount.note: This original term and URI from the OMA specification should be used: http://www.openmobilealliance.com/oma-dd/timed-count .
timedCount.definition: The number of seconds after which timed metering use of the asset begins.
timedCount.label: Timed Count

payAmount.definition: The amount of a financial payment. Right operand value MUST be an xsd:decimal.
payAmount.label: Payment Amount
payAmount.note: Can be used for compensation duties with the unit property indicating the currency of the payment.

device.definition: An identified device used for exercising the action of the Rule.
device.label: Device

meteredTime.label: Metered Time
meteredTime.definition: An accumulated amount of one to many metered time periods which were used for exercising the action of the Rule. Right operand value MUST be an xsd:duration as defined by [[xmlschema11-2]].
meteredTime.note:
Only the eq, lt, lteq operators SHOULD be used. See also Elapsed Time.
Example: meteredTime lteq P60M indicates an accumulated period of 60 Minutes or less.

derive.definition: To create a new derivative Asset from this Asset and to edit or modify the derivative.
derive.note: A new asset is created and may have significant overlaps with the original Asset. (Note that the notion of whether or not the change is significant enough to qualify as a new asset is subjective). To the derived Asset a next policy may be applied.
derive.label: Derive

dataType.definition: The datatype of the value of the rightOperand or rightOperandReference of a Constraint.
dataType.label: Datatype
dataType.note: In RDF encodings, use of the rdf:datatype MUST be used. In JSON-LD encoding, the use of @type MUST be used.

dateTime.note:
The use of Timezone information is strongly recommended. The Rule may be exercised before (with operator lt/lteq) or after (with operator gt/gteq) the date(time) defined by the Right operand.
Example: dateTime gteq 2017-12-31T06:00Z means the Rule can only be exercised after (and including) 6:00AM on the 31st Decemeber 2017 UTC time.
dateTime.label: Datetime
dateTime.definition: The date (and optional time and timezone) of exercising the action of the Rule. Right operand value MUST be an xsd:date or xsd:dateTime as defined by [[xmlschema11-2]].

fileFormat.note: Example: An asset may be transformed into JPEG format.
fileFormat.definition: A transformed file format of the target Asset.
fileFormat.label: File Format

remedy.label: Remedy
remedy.definition: Relates an individual remedy Duty to a Prohibition.
remedy.note: The remedy property expresses an agreed Duty that must be fulfilled in case that a Prohibition has been violated by being exercised.

read.label: Read
read.definition: To obtain data from the Asset.
read.note: For example, the ability to read a record from a database (the Asset).

SourceCode.note: This term is defined by Creative Commons.
SourceCode.label: Source Code
SourceCode.definition: Source code (the preferred form for making modifications) must be provided when exercising some rights granted by the license.

adHocShare.definition: The act of sharing the asset to parties in close proximity to the owner.
adHocShare.label: Ad-hoc sharing
adHocShare.note: This original term and URI from the OMA specification should be used: http://www.openmobilealliance.com/oma-dd/adhoc-share .

compensate.label: Compensate
compensate.definition: To compensate by transfer of some amount of value, if defined, for using or selling the Asset.
compensate.note: The compensation may use different types of things with a value: (i) the thing is expressed by the value (term) of the Constraint name; (b) the value is expressed by operator, rightOperand, dataType and unit. Typically the assignee will compensate the assigner, but other compensation party roles may be used.

inheritAllowed.definition: Indicates if the Policy entity can be inherited.
inheritAllowed.label: Inheritance Allowed

andSequence.note: This property MUST only be used for Logical Constraints, and the list of operand values MUST be Constraint instances. The order of the list MUST be preserved. The andSequence operator is an example where there may be temporal conditional requirements between the operands. This may lead to situations where the outcome is unresolvable, such as deadlock if one of the Constraints is unable to be satisfied. ODRL Processing systems SHOULD plan for these scenarios and implement mechanisms to resolve them.
andSequence.definition: The relation is satisfied when each of the Constraints are satisfied in the order specified.
andSequence.label: And Sequence

output.definition: The output property specifies the Asset which is created from the output of the Action.
output.label: Output

Policy.definition: A non-empty group of Permissions and/or Prohibitions.
Policy.note: A Policy may contain multiple Rules.
Policy.label: Policy

ConflictTerm.definition: Used to establish strategies to resolve conflicts that arise from the merging of Policies or conflicts between Permissions and Prohibitions in the same Policy.
ConflictTerm.note: Instances of ConflictTerm describe strategies for resolving conflicts.
ConflictTerm.label: Conflict Strategy Preference

Notice.note: This term is defined by Creative Commons.
Notice.definition: Copyright and license notices be kept intact.
Notice.label: Notice

neq.definition: Indicating that a given value is not equal to the right operand of the Constraint.
neq.label: Not equal to

purpose.label: Purpose
purpose.definition: A defined purpose for exercising the action of the Rule.

partOf.definition: Identifies an Asset/PartyCollection that the Asset/Party is a member of.
partOf.label: Part Of

secondaryUse.label: Secondary Use
secondaryUse.definition: The act of using the asset for a purpose other than the purpose it was intended for.

event.definition: An identified event setting a context for exercising the action of the Rule.
event.note:
Events are temporal periods of time, and operators can be used to signal before (lt), during (eq) or after (gt) the event.
Example: May be taken during the “FIFA World Cup 2020” only.
event.label: Event

textToSpeech.note: If the action is to be performed to a wider audience than just the Assignees, then the recipient constraint is recommended to be used.
textToSpeech.label: Text-to-speech
textToSpeech.definition: To have a text Asset read out loud.

Reproduction.label: Reproduction
Reproduction.note: This term is defined by Creative Commons.

Sharing.note: This term is defined by Creative Commons.
Sharing.label: Sharing
Sharing.definition: Permits commercial derivatives, but only non-commercial distribution.

anonymize.definition: To anonymize all or parts of the Asset.
anonymize.note: For example, to remove identifying particulars for statistical or for other comparable purposes, or to use the Asset without stating the author/source.
anonymize.label: Anonymize

attachSource.definition: The act of attaching the source of the asset and its derivatives.
attachSource.label: Attach source

and.note: This property MUST only be used for Logical Constraints, and the list of operand values MUST be Constraint instances.
and.definition: The relation is satisfied when all of the Constraints are satisfied.
and.label: And

Asset.label: Asset
Asset.note: The Asset entity can be any form of identifiable resource, such as data/information, content/media, applications, or services. Furthermore, it can be used to represent other Asset entities that are needed to undertake the Policy expression, such as with the Duty entity. To describe more details about the Asset, it is recommended to use Dublin Core [[dcterms]] elements or other content metadata.
Asset.definition: A resource or a collection of resources that are the subject of a Rule.

scope.label: Scope
scope.definition: The identifier of a scope that provides context to the extent of the entity.
scope.note: Used to define scopes for Assets and Parties.

xone.definition: The relation is satisfied when only one, and not more, of the Constaints is satisfied
xone.label: Only One
xone.note: This property MUST only be used for Logical Constraints, and the list of operand values MUST be Constraint instances.

modify.definition: To change existing content of the Asset. A new asset is not created by this action.
modify.note: This action will modify an asset which is typically updated from time to time without creating a new asset. If the result from modifying the asset should be a new asset the actions derive or extract should be used. (Note that the notion of whether or not the change is significant enough to qualify as a new asset is subjective).
modify.label: Modify

timeInterval.definition: A recurring period of time before the next execution of the action of the Rule. Right operand value MUST be an xsd:duration as defined by [[xmlschema11-2]].
timeInterval.label: Recurring Time Interval
timeInterval.note:
Only the eq operator SHOULD be used.
Example: timeInterval eq P7D indicates a recurring 7 day period.

PartyScope.label: Party Scope
PartyScope.note: Instances of the PartyScope class represent the terms for the scope property of Parties.

failure.definition: Failure is an abstract property that defines the violation (or unmet) relationship between Rules.
failure.note: The parent property to sub-properties that express explicit failure contexts.
failure.label: Failure

constraint.label: Has Constraint
constraint.note: Constraints on Rules are used to determine if a rule is Active or not. Example: the Permission rule is only active during the year 2018.

Individual.label: Individual
Individual.definition: Specifies that the scope of the relationship is the single Party individual.

UndefinedTerm.definition: Is used to indicate how to support Actions that are not part of any vocabulary or profile in the policy expression system.
UndefinedTerm.label: Undefined Term
UndefinedTerm.note: Instances of UndefinedTerm describe strategies for processing unsupported actions.

isNoneOf.label: Is none of
isNoneOf.definition: A set-based operator indicating that a given value is none of the right operand of the Constraint.

play.note: For example, to play a video or audio track. If the action is to be performed to a wider audience than just the Assignees, then the Recipient constraint is recommended to be used.
play.label: Play
play.definition: To create a sequential and transient rendition of an Asset.

move.definition: To move the Asset from one digital location to another including deleting the original copy.
move.note: After the Asset has been moved, the original copy must be deleted.
move.label: Move

isPartOf.definition: A set-based operator indicating that a given value is contained by the right operand of the Constraint.
isPartOf.label: Is part of

action.label: Has Action
action.definition: The operation relating to the Asset for which the Rule is being subjected.

lt.label: Less than
lt.definition: Indicating that a given value is less than the right operand of the Constraint.

elapsedTime.definition: A continuous elapsed time period which may be used for exercising of the action of the Rule. Right operand value MUST be an xsd:duration as defined by [[xmlschema11-2]].
elapsedTime.label: Elapsed Time
elapsedTime.note:
Only the eq, lt, lteq operators SHOULD be used. See also Metered Time.
Example: elpasedTime eq P60M indicates a total elapsed time of 60 Minutes.

append.label: Append
append.definition: The act of adding to the end of an asset.

policyUsage.definition: Indicates the actual datetime the action of the Rule was exercised.
policyUsage.note:
This can be used to express constraints with a LeftOperand relative to the time the rule is exercised. Operators indicate before (lt, lteq), during (eq) or after (gt, gteq) the usage of the rule.
Example: event lt policyUsage expresses that the identified event must have happened before the action of the rule is exercised.
policyUsage.label: Policy Rule Usage

All.label: All
All.note: For example, may be used to indicate all the users of a specific social network the party is a member of. Note that “group” scope is also assumed.
All.definition: Specifies that the scope of the relationship is all of the collective individuals within a context.

unitOfCount.definition: The unit of measure used for counting the executions of the action of the Rule.
unitOfCount.note:
Note: Typically used with Duties to indicate the unit entity to be counted of the Action.
Example: A duty to compensate and a unitOfCount constraint of 'perUser' would indicate that the compensation by multiplied by the 'number of users'.
unitOfCount.label: Unit Of Count

preview.definition: The act of providing a short preview of the asset.
preview.label: Preview
preview.note: Use a time constraint with the appropriate action.

delete.label: Delete
delete.definition: To permanently remove all copies of the Asset after it has been used.
delete.note: Use a constraint to define under which conditions the Asset must be deleted.

prohibit.note: Used to determine policy conflict outcomes.
prohibit.label: Prefer Prohibitions

share.label: Share
share.definition: The act of the non-commercial reproduction and distribution of the asset to third-parties.

shareAlike.label: Share-alike
shareAlike.definition: The act of distributing any derivative asset under the same terms as the original asset.

assignee.definition: The Party is the recipient of the Rule.
assignee.label: Assignee

RightOperand.label: Right Operand
RightOperand.note: Instances of the RightOperand class are used as the rightOperand of a Constraint.

perm.label: Prefer Permissions
perm.note: Used to determine policy conflict outcomes.
